1	name: CI
2	on:
3	  # Run CI for PRs to `main` and to release branches.
4	  #
5	  # Note that PRs to `main` will run a subset of tests and PRs to the
6	  # `release-*` branches will run full CI.
7	  pull_request:
8	    branches:
9	    - main
10	    - 'release-*'
11	
12	  # This is the CI that runs for PRs-to-merge.
13	  merge_group:
14	
15	  push:
16	    branches:
17	    # Right now merge queues can't be used with wildcards in branch protections
18	    # so full CI runs both on PRs to release branches as well as merges to
19	    # release branches. Note that the merge to a release branch may produce a
20	    # tag at the end of CI if successful and the tag will trigger the artifact
21	    # uploads as well as publication to crates.io.
22	    - 'release-*'
23	
24	  # Allow manually triggering this request via a button or another workflow.
25	  workflow_dispatch:
26	
27	defaults:
28	  run:
29	    shell: bash
30	
31	# Cancel any in-flight jobs for the same PR/branch so there's only one active
32	# at a time
33	concurrency:
34	  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}2
35	  cancel-in-progress: true
36	
37	jobs:
38	  # Check Code style quickly by running `rustfmt` over all code
39	  rustfmt:
40	    name: Rustfmt
41	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
42	    steps:
43	    - uses: actions/checkout@v6
44	      with:
45	        submodules: true
46	    - uses: ./.github/actions/install-rust
47	    - run: rustup component add rustfmt
48	    - run: cargo fmt --all -- --check
49	
50	  # Quick JS formatting/linting checks for the little bits of JS we have for the
51	  # `wasmtime explore` UI.
52	  check_js:
53	    name: Check JS
54	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
55	    steps:
56	    - uses: actions/checkout@v6
57	    - run: npm install
58	      working-directory: ./crates/explorer
59	    - run: npm run lint
60	      working-directory: ./crates/explorer
61	    - run: npm run fmt-check
62	      working-directory: ./crates/explorer
63	
64	  # Check Code style quickly by running `clang-format` over all the C/C++ code
65	  #
66	  # Note that `wasmtime-platform.h` is excluded here as it's auto-generated.
67	  clangformat:
68	    name: Clang format
69	    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
70	    steps:
71	    - uses: actions/checkout@v6
72	      with:
73	        submodules: true
74	    - run: |
75	        git ls-files '*.h' '*.c' '*.cpp' '*.hh' '*.cc' | \
76	          grep -v wasmtime-platform.h | \
77	          grep -v wasm.h | \
78	          xargs clang-format-18 --dry-run --Werror --verbose
79	    # Make sure our `clangformat.sh` helper script keeps running and stays
80	    # in-sync with the previous check.
81	    - run: crates/c-api/clangformat.sh
82	    - run: git diff --exit-code
83	
84	  # Lint dependency graph for security advisories, duplicate versions, and
85	  # incompatible licences
86	  cargo_deny:
87	    name: Cargo deny
88	    needs: determine
89	    if: needs.determine.outputs.audit
90	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
91	    steps:
92	    - uses: actions/checkout@v6
93	      with:
94	        submodules: true
95	    - uses: ./.github/actions/install-rust
96	    - uses: ./.github/actions/cargo-install
97	      with:
98	        crate: cargo-deny
99	        version: 0.18.2
100	    - run: cargo deny check bans licenses
101	
102	  # Ensure dependencies are vetted. See https://mozilla.github.io/cargo-vet/
103	  #
104	  # Note that this step, on PRs only, is allowed to fail. This is then followed
105	  # up with the `cargo_vet_failure_for_prs` step below. The intention is to
106	  # avoid causing this check to fail PRs while still enabling it to fail the
107	  # merge queue checks. That way PRs can enter the merge queue when this step is
108	  # failing if `main` has picked up `cargo vet` entries in the meantime for the
109	  # failures.
110	  cargo_vet:
111	    name: Cargo vet
112	    needs: determine
113	    if: github.repository == 'bytecodealliance/wasmtime' && needs.determine.outputs.audit
114	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
115	    outputs:
116	      outcome: ${{ steps.vet.outcome }}
117	    steps:
118	    - uses: actions/checkout@v6
119	      with:
120	        submodules: true
121	    - uses: ./.github/actions/install-rust
122	    - uses: ./.github/actions/cargo-install
123	      with:
124	        crate: cargo-vet
125	        version: 0.10.2
126	    - id: vet
127	      run: cargo vet --locked
128	      continue-on-error: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}
129	
130	    # Double-check that if versions are bumped that `cargo vet` still works.
131	    # This is intended to weed out mistakes such as #9115 from happening again.
132	    - run: rustc scripts/publish.rs && ./publish bump-patch && cargo vet
133	      name: Ensure `cargo vet` works if versions are bumped
134	
135	  cargo_vet_failure_for_prs:
136	    name: Cargo vet failed on a Pull Request
137	    needs:
138	    - determine
139	    - cargo_vet
140	    if: |
141	      needs.determine.outputs.audit
142	      && github.event_name == 'pull_request'
143	      && needs.cargo_vet.outputs.outcome == 'failure'
144	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
145	    steps:
146	    # NB: this message ideally would link back to the previous step, but I'm not
147	    # sure how to easily do that.
148	    - run: |
149	        echo 'failed to run "cargo vet", see previous `Cargo vet` step'
150	        echo 'exiting with a nonzero status now to alert PR authors'
151	        echo 'note, though, that this PR can still enter the merge queue'
152	        echo ''
153	        echo 'See https://docs.wasmtime.dev/contributing-coding-guidelines.html#cargo-vet-for-contributors'
154	        echo 'for more information about the vetting process for Wasmtime'
155	        exit 1
156	
157	  # This job is a dependency of many of the jobs below. This calculates what's
158	  # actually being run for this workflow. For example:
159	  #
160	  # * Pushes to branches, which is currently both pushes to merge queue branches
161	  #   as well as release branches, perform full CI.
162	  # * PRs to release branches (not `main`) run full CI.
163	  # * PRs to `main` will only run a few smoke tests above plus some elements of
164	  #   the test matrix. The test matrix here is determined dynamically by the
165	  #   `./ci/build-test-matrix.js` script given the commits that happened and
166	  #   the files modified.
167	  determine:
168	    name: Determine CI jobs to run
169	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
170	    outputs:
171	      run-full: ${{ steps.calculate.outputs.run-full }}
172	      test-matrix: ${{ steps.calculate.outputs.test-matrix }}
173	      build-matrix: ${{ steps.calculate.outputs.build-matrix }}
174	      test-capi: ${{ steps.calculate.outputs.test-capi }}
175	      test-nightly: ${{ steps.calculate.outputs.test-nightly }}
176	      test-miri: ${{ steps.calculate.outputs.test-miri }}
177	      audit: ${{ steps.calculate.outputs.audit }}
178	      preview1-adapter: ${{ steps.calculate.outputs.preview1-adapter }}
179	      run-dwarf: ${{ steps.calculate.outputs.run-dwarf }}
180	      platform-checks: ${{ steps.calculate.outputs.platform-checks }}
181	      test-gc-zeal: ${{ steps.calculate.outputs.test-gc-zeal }}
182	    steps:
183	    - uses: actions/checkout@v6
184	    - id: calculate
185	      env:
186	        GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
187	      run: |
188	        touch commits.log names.log
189	        # Note that CI doesn't run on pushes to `main`, only pushes to merge
190	        # queue branches and release branches, so this only runs full CI in
191	        # those locations.
192	        if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" != "pull_request" ]; then
193	          run_full=true
194	        else
195	          pr=${{ github.event.number }}
196	          gh pr view $pr --json commits | tee commits.log
197	          gh pr diff $pr --name-only | tee names.log || echo "failed to get files"
198	          if [ "${{ github.base_ref }}" != "main" ]; then
199	            run_full=true
200	          elif grep -q 'prtest:full' commits.log; then
201	            run_full=true
202	          elif grep -q 'prtest:debug' commits.log; then
203	            echo run-dwarf=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
204	          elif grep -q 'prtest:platform-checks' commits.log; then
205	            echo platform-checks=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
206	          elif grep -q 'prtest:miri' commits.log; then
207	            echo test-miri=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
208	          elif grep -q 'prtest:capi' commits.log; then
209	            echo test-capi=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
210	          fi
211	          if grep -q crates.c-api names.log; then
212	            echo test-capi=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
213	          fi
214	          if grep -q fuzz names.log; then
215	            echo test-nightly=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
216	          fi
217	          if grep -q sys.custom names.log; then
218	            echo test-nightly=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
219	          fi
220	          if grep -q Cargo.lock names.log; then
221	            echo audit=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
222	          fi
223	          if grep -q supply-chain names.log; then
224	            echo audit=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
225	          fi
226	          if grep -q component-adapter names.log; then
227	            echo preview1-adapter=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
228	          fi
229	          if grep -q debug names.log; then
230	            echo run-dwarf=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
231	          fi
232	          if grep -q pulley names.log; then
233	            echo test-nightly=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
234	          fi
235	          if grep -q gc names.log; then
236	            echo test-gc-zeal=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
237	          fi
238	        fi
239	        matrix="$(node ./ci/build-test-matrix.js ./commits.log ./names.log $run_full)"
240	        echo "test-matrix={\"include\":$(echo $matrix)}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
241	        echo "$matrix"
242	
243	        matrix="$(node ./ci/build-build-matrix.js)"
244	        echo "build-matrix={\"include\":$(echo $matrix)}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
245	
246	        if [ "$run_full" = "true" ]; then
247	            echo run-full=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
248	            echo test-capi=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
249	            echo test-nightly=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
250	            echo test-miri=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
251	            echo test-gc-zeal=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
252	            echo audit=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
253	            echo preview1-adapter=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
254	            echo run-dwarf=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
255	            echo platform-checks=true >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

1022	    - name: Configure OpenSSL (Windows)
1023	      if: runner.os == 'Windows'
1024	      shell: pwsh
1025	      run: |
1026	        mkdir "C:\Program Files\OpenSSL\certs"
1027	        $sslCertFile = "C:\Program Files\OpenSSL\certs\cacert.pem"
1028	        $opensslDir = "C:\Program Files\OpenSSL"
1029	        $opensslLibDir = "C:\Program Files\OpenSSL\lib\VC\x64\MD"
1030	        curl.exe -o $sslCertFile https://curl.se/ca/cacert.pem
1031	        "SSL_CERT_FILE=$sslCertFile" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Append
1032	        "OPENSSL_DIR=$opensslDir" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Append
1033	        "OPENSSL_LIB_DIR=$opensslLibDir" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Append
1034	    - run: cargo test -p wasmtime-wasi-tls --all-features
